latest.title: Faron Pharmaceuticals: Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd: Registration of Treasury Shares
latest.content:
Today 15:00
Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd | Company announcement | June 11, 2026 at 17:00:00 EEST
Capitalised terms used in this announcement have the meanings given to them in the announcement made on 28 May 2026 at 15:35 EEST regarding the issue of treasury shares to the Company, unless the context provides otherwise.
Turku, Finland
– Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing novel immunotherapies, as announced on 28 May 2026, issued 21,469,427 treasury shares to the Company itself without consideration to further prepare for any future conversions of the First and Second Tranche of Bonds.
In total, 21,469,427 new Treasury Shares in the Company have on 8 June 2026 been registered in the Finnish Trade Register. The treasury shares will rank pari passu in all respects with the existing shares of the Company. Following the registration of new Shares, the aggregate number of ordinary shares in the Company is 227,881,315 and of these shares, the Company holds 25,000,000 shares in treasury. The shares held in treasury by the Company do not confer a right to dividends or other shareholder rights.
The treasury shares will be listed on First North on 12 June 2026 and on AIM on or around 17 June 2026.
For more information, please contact:
IR Partners, Finland
(Media)
Kare Laukkanen
+358 50 553 9535 / +44 7 469 766 223
kare.laukkanen@irpartners.fi
FINN Partners, US
(Media)
Alyssa Paldo
+1 847 791-8085
alyssa.paldo@finnpartners.com
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP
(Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Sandy Jamieson, Jo Turner
+44 (0) 207 213 0880
Sisu Partners Oy
(Certified Adviser on Nasdaq First North)
Juha Karttunen
Jukka Järvelä
+358 (0)40 555 4727
+358 (0)50 553 8990
About Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Faron (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON) is a global, clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focused on tackling cancers via novel immunotherapies. Its mission is to bring the promise of immunotherapy to a broader population by uncovering novel ways to control and harness the power of the immune system. The Company’s lead asset is bexmarilimab, a novel anti-Clever-1 humanized antibody, with the potential to remove immunosuppression of cancers through reprogramming myeloid cell function. Bexmarilimab is being investigated in Phase I/II clinical trials as a potential therapy for patients with hematological cancers in combination with other standard treatments. Further information is available at
www.faron.com.

company_30s.what_they_do: Faron Pharmaceuticals is a small‑cap biotech that focuses on discovering and developing novel therapies for rare genetic disorders and oncology indications.  Its pipeline includes a lead candidate in late‑stage clinical trials and several early‑stage assets targeting high‑need therapeutic areas.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The biotech arena is worth billions, and companies that can secure a first‑in‑class drug for a rare disease often attract large partnerships or acquisitions.  Faron’s lead candidate sits in a niche where competition is limited and the commercial potential is substantial if the drug gains regulatory approval.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a survival mode: it has just completed a €40 million rights issue, issued new shares, and is under review by Nasdaq Helsinki’s disciplinary committee.  Trading was recently suspended, and the company is actively raising capital while dealing with regulatory scrutiny, suggesting a cash‑burn trajectory with high uncertainty.

rns_narrative: Regulatory & Capital‑Raising Phase: Faron has issued new shares and completed a rights issue to shore up its balance sheet, but its listing on Nasdaq Helsinki is under review and trading was suspended.  The company is also attempting to align its market release times to avoid further volatility.
Leadership & Strategic Moves: A new CTO was appointed to strengthen late‑stage development, signalling an intent to accelerate the drug‑development timeline.
Stabilization Efforts: The company is actively addressing the disciplinary findings and working to restore confidence among investors and regulators.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None – the few bullish posts focus on the new CTO and potential funding, but they are not widespread.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Regulatory risk, a suspended trading status, and a heavy cash burn create a narrative that the company may fail to regain listing status or secure further capital.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially silent, the sentiment has shifted to cautious optimism after the leadership change, yet remains largely negative due to ongoing regulatory uncertainty.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public buzz, which means a catalyst could produce a surprise spike.

near_term_catalysts: Resolution of the Nasdaq Helsinki disciplinary decision – a positive ruling could lift the trading suspension and restore liquidity.
Completion of the rights‑issue and share registration – a successful capital raise would reduce cash burn and signal confidence.
Announcement of new clinical data for the lead oncology candidate – positive results would attract institutional interest.
Reinstatement of trading on the London and Helsinki exchanges – increased volume would support a rebound.
expect_next.watch_for: A clear regulatory decision, a successful funding round, or a breakthrough trial report.  Volume spikes above 10 000 shares on news releases are key confirmation signals.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the late‑stage washout of a deep crash.  Capitulation is likely complete; the APEX score shows tight compression, suggesting mean reversion is imminent once a catalyst resolves the regulatory impasse.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company sits at a deep crash bottom, historically the most rewarding zone for the system’s GREEN signals (average 39% return).  If the price recovers to halfway between its current level and the 52‑week high, the upside is roughly 60% – a reward that far outweighs the modest downside risk.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Regulatory uncertainty, a suspended listing, and a heavy cash burn could lead to further decline or even delisting.  The worst‑case loss is a 40% drop to the 52‑week low if the company fails to secure additional funding or clear the regulatory hurdle.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside 60% versus a 40% downside – a 1.5:1 reward‑to‑risk ratio that favors a contrarian long stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 52‑week low (~0.30 p) after a positive catalyst, allocate 10 % of capital, and set a stop 20 % below entry.  Scale in as volume confirms the move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a clear regulatory decision or trial data release; watch for a volume surge above 10 000 shares on the news day as a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals, so the contrarian thesis remains intact.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash, regulatory resolution pending, and a 60 % upside for a 40 % downside make this a high‑reward contrarian play if the catalyst clears the current roadblock.
